title: 1401. In which year was the first passenger train in India started?
hint: It ran between Bombay and Thane.
summary: The first passenger train in India started in 1853 between Bombay (Mumbai) and Thane.

title: 1402. The headquarters of Indian Railways is located in which city?
hint: It is the national capital of India.
summary: Railway Board headquarters is located in New Delhi.

title: 1403. Which is the largest railway zone of Indian Railways by route length?
hint: It is headquartered in Jaipur.
summary: Northern Railway is among the largest railway zones by route length.

title: 1404. Which was the first railway zone created after the reorganisation of Indian Railways?
hint: It was created in 1951.
summary: Southern Railway was the first railway zone of independent India.

title: 1405. Which Indian railway station is known as the busiest railway station in India?
hint: It is located in Maharashtra.
summary: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus is one of India's busiest railway stations.

title: 1406. Which is the fastest train in India as of recent years?
hint: It is an indigenous semi-high-speed train.
summary: Vande Bharat Express is among the fastest trains operated by Indian Railways.

title: 1407. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Mumbai CST?
hint: It was formed in 1951.
summary: Central Railway headquarters is located at Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus, Mumbai.

title: 1408. Which railway line connects Mumbai and Thane?
hint: It was India's first passenger railway route.
summary: The first passenger train ran between Bori Bunder and Thane.

title: 1409. Which ministry controls Indian Railways?
hint: It is a separate ministry of the Government of India.
summary: The Ministry of Railways manages Indian Railways.

title: 1410. Which is the first railway station in India to receive ISO certification?
hint: It is located in Rajasthan.
summary: Jaipur Railway Station was among the first Indian railway stations to receive ISO certification.

title: 1411. Which was the first electric train introduced in India?
hint: It started between Bombay VT and Kurla.
summary: India's first electric train started in 1925 between Bombay VT and Kurla.

title: 1412. How many railway zones are currently operated by Indian Railways?
hint: The number includes the newest zone South Coast Railway.
summary: Indian Railways has 19 railway zones.

title: 1413. Which railway zone has headquarters at Gorakhpur?
hint: It has one of the largest route networks among railway zones.
summary: North Eastern Railway headquarters is located at Gorakhpur.

title: 1414. Which is the longest railway platform in India?
hint: It is located in Karnataka.
summary: Hubli (Shree Siddharoodha Swamiji Hubballi Junction) is the longest railway platform in India and the world.

title: 1415. Which railway zone operates in the Konkan region?
hint: It connects Maharashtra, Goa and Karnataka coastal areas.
summary: Konkan Railway Corporation operates the Konkan railway route.

title: 1416. Which train was the first Rajdhani Express introduced in India?
hint: It connected Delhi with West Bengal.
summary: The first Rajdhani Express started between New Delhi and Howrah in 1969.

title: 1417. Which railway station has a UNESCO World Heritage Site status?
hint: It is located in Mumbai.
summary: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: 1418. Which railway engine type is based on electric traction?
hint: It uses electricity instead of diesel fuel.
summary: Electric locomotives use electric traction.

title: 1419. Which railway zone has headquarters at New Delhi?
hint: It is one of the oldest railway zones.
summary: Northern Railway headquarters is located at New Delhi.

title: 1420. Which railway train is known as India's first semi-high-speed train?
hint: It was introduced before Vande Bharat.
summary: Gatimaan Express is India's first semi-high-speed train.

title: 1421. Which organization is responsible for manufacturing Vande Bharat trains in India?
hint: It is an Indian Railways production unit located in Chennai.
summary: Vande Bharat trains are manufactured by the Integral Coach Factory (ICF), Chennai.

title: 1422. Which gauge is the most widely used on the Indian Railways network?
hint: It has a width of 1,676 mm.
summary: Broad Gauge (1,676 mm) is the standard and most widely used gauge in India.

title: 1423. The Konkan Railway primarily connects which two major Indian cities?
hint: It runs along India's western coast.
summary: The Konkan Railway provides a direct rail link between Mumbai and Mangaluru along the west coast.

title: 1424. Which production unit of Indian Railways manufactures electric locomotives at Madhepura under a joint venture?
hint: It manufactures high-horsepower electric locomotives.
summary: The Madhepura Electric Locomotive Factory manufactures high-power electric locomotives.

title: 1425. Which railway production unit is located at Chittaranjan in West Bengal?
hint: It is one of India's oldest locomotive manufacturing units.
summary: Chittaranjan Locomotive Works is a major producer of electric locomotives.

title: 1426. Which Indian Railways production unit is located at Kapurthala, Punjab?
hint: It primarily manufactures passenger coaches.
summary: Rail Coach Factory (RCF), Kapurthala is a major coach manufacturing unit of Indian Railways.

title: 1427. Which city is the headquarters of the South Central Railway zone?
hint: It is the capital of Telangana.
summary: South Central Railway has its headquarters at Secunderabad.

title: 1428. Which luxury tourist train is jointly operated by Rajasthan Tourism and Indian Railways?
hint: It is named after a historic royal palace.
summary: The Palace on Wheels is a luxury tourist train jointly operated by Rajasthan Tourism and Indian Railways.

title: 1429. Which railway station serves as the headquarters of the East Central Railway?
hint: It is located in Bihar.
summary: East Central Railway is headquartered at Hajipur in Bihar.

title: 1430. Which Indian Railways organization is responsible for railway electrification projects?
hint: It is a public sector enterprise under the Ministry of Railways.
summary: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL) executes railway infrastructure and electrification projects.

title: 1431. Which organization operates the online ticket booking portal of Indian Railways?
hint: It is a PSU under the Ministry of Railways.
summary: IRCTC operates the online ticket booking portal and provides catering and tourism services.

title: 1432. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Kolkata?
hint: It is one of the oldest railway zones.
summary: Eastern Railway is headquartered at Fairlie Place, Kolkata.

title: 1433. Which is the first privately operated train introduced in India under Indian Railways?
hint: It runs between Lucknow and New Delhi.
summary: Tejas Express (Lucknow–New Delhi) became India's first privately operated train by IRCTC.

title: 1434. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Bhubaneswar?
hint: It primarily serves Odisha.
summary: East Coast Railway is headquartered at Bhubaneswar.

title: 1435. Which Indian Railways PSU specializes in railway engineering and consultancy services?
hint: Its full form is Rail India Technical and Economic Service.
summary: RITES is India's leading transport infrastructure consultancy PSU.

title: 1436. Which city is the headquarters of the West Central Railway zone?
hint: It is known as the 'Sanskardhani' of Madhya Pradesh.
summary: West Central Railway is headquartered at Jabalpur.

title: 1437. Which railway PSU is mainly responsible for executing railway projects in India and abroad?
hint: Its name begins with 'Indian Railway Construction'.
summary: IRCON International executes railway and infrastructure projects in India and overseas.

title: 1438. Which city is the headquarters of the North Central Railway zone?
hint: It is also known as Prayagraj.
summary: North Central Railway is headquartered at Prayagraj.

title: 1439. Which organization owns and operates the Dedicated Freight Corridors in India?
hint: It is a PSU under the Ministry of Railways.
summary: Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL) develops and operates freight corridors.

title: 1440. Which Indian Railways production unit is located at Raebareli, Uttar Pradesh?
hint: It manufactures modern passenger coaches.
summary: Modern Coach Factory (MCF), Raebareli manufactures LHB and other modern coaches.

title: 1441. Which organization is the research and development wing of Indian Railways?
hint: It is headquartered in Lucknow.
summary: Research Designs and Standards Organisation (RDSO) is the R&D organization of Indian Railways.

title: 1442. Where is the headquarters of the Northeast Frontier Railway located?
hint: It is the largest city of Assam.
summary: The Northeast Frontier Railway is headquartered at Maligaon, Guwahati.

title: 1443. Banaras Locomotive Works (BLW) is located in which city?
hint: It was formerly known as Diesel Locomotive Works.
summary: Banaras Locomotive Works is located in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh.

title: 1444. Which production unit manufactures railway wheels and axles?
hint: It is located at Yelahanka near Bengaluru.
summary: Rail Wheel Factory manufactures wheels, axles and wheelsets for Indian Railways.

title: 1445. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Mumbai (Churchgate)?
hint: It serves western India.
summary: Western Railway is headquartered at Churchgate, Mumbai.

title: 1446. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Hubballi?
hint: It serves Karnataka and parts of Goa.
summary: South Western Railway is headquartered at Hubballi, Karnataka.

title: 1447. Which Indian Railways coach type is named after Linke Hofmann Busch?
hint: It is used in most premium long-distance trains.
summary: LHB coaches are based on German Linke Hofmann Busch technology.

title: 1448. Which railway zone has its headquarters at Bilaspur?
hint: It was created in 2003.
summary: South East Central Railway is headquartered at Bilaspur.

title: 1449. Which railway production unit at Chennai manufactured the first Vande Bharat (Train 18)?
hint: It is India's oldest coach manufacturing unit.
summary: The first Vande Bharat train was manufactured at Integral Coach Factory, Chennai.

title: 1450. Which ministry administers Indian Railways?
hint: It is a separate ministry of the Government of India.
summary: Indian Railways functions under the Ministry of Railways.

options.title: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways
